In C# it is possible to estimate the time taken for a UDP packet to reach a server  
using the System.Net.Sockets namespace  
you can send a datagram and then wait for a response  
however UDP does not guarantee delivery or response  
so this approach may not always work  
you can use a Stopwatch to measure the time between sending and receiving  
if a response is received  
you can calculate the time difference  
this is not exactly like ping  
because ping measures round trip time  
but it can give an approximate idea of one way delay  
you need to handle exceptions and timeouts carefully  
this method is not reliable for all scenarios  
but it can be used in some cases  
you can use the UdpClient class to send and receive packets  
you can send a packet and then wait for a reply  
the time taken for the packet to reach the server and return is measured  
this is not a one way measurement  
but it can be used to estimate the delay  
you can use the Send and Receive methods of the UdpClient class  
you can use a Stopwatch to measure the time between sending and receiving  
this can give an approximate idea of the delay
